title: calamintha graveolens
intro: Calamintha graveolens, also known as Clinopodium graveolens or wild basil, is a perennial herb that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calamintha graveolens include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and providing relief from headaches and fever. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
